subject: Writing
audience: Middle and high school students learning to write clearer essays

units.name: Idea Forge
units.metaphor: Turning raw opinions into precise claims
units.description: Students learn to name what they mean before they defend it.

units.name: Evidence Workbench
units.metaphor: Choosing proof that actually carries the argument
units.description: Students connect evidence to claims with interpretation.

units.name: Revision Loop
units.metaphor: Making the draft stronger each time it breaks
units.description: Students learn that better writing is built through retries.

concepts.name: Clear Thesis
concepts.description: A specific, arguable main claim that gives the essay direction.
concepts.story: A thesis is the compass for the whole essay. If it points vaguely, every paragraph wanders.
concepts.grit_prompt: A fuzzy thesis is not failure. It is the first signal that your argument wants sharper edges.
concepts.visual_metaphor: A bright compass needle locking onto one destination.

ingredients.label: Arguable claim
ingredients.description: The thesis takes a position someone could reasonably challenge.
ingredients.failure_mode: The student writes a fact or broad topic instead of a claim.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Rewrite a broad topic as a claim that can be defended.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: A statement turning from mist into a carved signpost.

ingredients.label: Scope control
ingredients.description: The claim is narrow enough to prove in the essay length.
ingredients.failure_mode: The student tries to prove too much at once.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Shrink an oversized thesis until it fits three body paragraphs.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: A camera lens zooming from a skyline to one building.

concepts.name: Evidence Selection
concepts.description: Choosing quotes, examples, or facts that directly support the thesis.
concepts.story: Evidence is not decoration. It is the load-bearing beam in the paragraph.
concepts.grit_prompt: When evidence does not fit, you are learning what your argument truly needs.
concepts.visual_metaphor: A bridge beam clicking into place between claim and reader.

ingredients.label: Relevance check
ingredients.description: The evidence answers the exact claim, not a nearby idea.
ingredients.failure_mode: The evidence is interesting but not useful.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Sort evidence into supports, distracts, or needs explanation.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: Magnets snapping only to matching pieces.

concepts.name: Commentary Bridge
concepts.description: Explaining how evidence proves the claim.
concepts.story: Commentary is where the student becomes the thinker, not just the collector of quotes.
concepts.grit_prompt: If your explanation feels hard, that is the workout. Stay with the connection.
concepts.visual_metaphor: A lit path from a quote to the thesis.

ingredients.label: Because chain
ingredients.description: The student uses because-reasoning to connect evidence to meaning.
ingredients.failure_mode: The paragraph drops evidence and moves on.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Add two because-statements after a quote.

concepts.name: Revision from Feedback
concepts.description: Using critique to make a specific next draft stronger.
concepts.story: Feedback is not a verdict. It is a map of where the essay can grow next.
concepts.grit_prompt: A returned draft is not the end of the work. It is the moment the work gets smarter.
concepts.visual_metaphor: A draft page gaining new routes and stronger foundations.

ingredients.label: Targeted change
ingredients.description: The student turns feedback into one concrete edit.
ingredients.failure_mode: The student rereads feedback but does not change the draft.
ingredients.practice_prompt: Convert a teacher comment into one exact sentence-level revision.
ingredients.visual_metaphor: A repair tool tightening one loose bolt.
